actor_claims.actor_label: person or group from whom the Company received a written alternative proposal
actor_claims.quote_text: each person or group from whom the Company received a written alternative proposal

event_claims.description: Until 12:01 a.m. New York time on September 6, 2013, the Company and related parties were permitted to initiate, solicit or encourage alternative proposals.
event_claims.quote_text: Pursuant to the merger agreement, until 12:01 a.m., New York time, on September 6, 2013 (the “go shop period”), the Company and its subsidiaries, and their respective representatives, were permitted to:

event_claims.description: During the go-shop period, the Company and related parties were permitted to solicit inquiries, proposals or offers constituting alternative proposals, including by providing information and access under confidentiality agreements.
event_claims.quote_text: initiate, solicit or encourage any inquiry or the making of any proposal or offer that constitutes an alternative proposal

event_claims.description: During the go-shop period, the Company and related parties were permitted to participate in discussions or negotiations regarding alternative proposals.
event_claims.quote_text: engage in, enter into, continue or otherwise participate in any discussions or negotiations with any person or group of persons with respect to any alternative proposals

event_claims.description: The Company was required to notify Hudson’s Bay of the identity of each person or group submitting a written alternative proposal before the end of the go-shop period.
event_claims.quote_text: The Company was required to promptly (and in any event within two business days) notify Hudson’s Bay of the identity of each person or group from whom the Company received a written alternative proposal after the execution of the merger agreement and prior to 12:01 a.m., New York time, on September 6, 2013

event_claims.description: Within three business days of September 6, 2013, the Company was required to provide Hudson’s Bay a list of persons or groups whose written alternative proposals could reasonably be expected to result in a superior proposal, defined as excluded parties.
event_claims.quote_text: within three business days of September 6, 2013, the Company was required to provide Hudson’s Bay with a list of persons or groups from whom the Company or its representatives received prior to 12:01 a.m., New York time, on September 6, 2013, a written alternative proposal that the board determined in good faith, after consultation with outside counsel and financial advisors, was or could reasonably be expected to result in, a superior proposal (each, an “excluded party”).

event_claims.description: On September 10, 2013, the Company announced that the go-shop period expired on September 6, 2013 and no party had been designated as an excluded party.
event_claims.quote_text: On September 10, 2013, the Company announced that the go shop period expired on September 6, 2013, and no party had been designated by the Company as an excluded party.

event_claims.description: Since 12:01 a.m. New York time on September 6, 2013, the Company and related parties were required to cease solicitation activities and discussions or negotiations relating to alternative proposals, subject to excluded-party and other permitted exceptions.
event_claims.quote_text: since 12:01 a.m., New York time, on September 6, 2013, the Company and its subsidiaries have been and are required to, and the Company has been and is required to cause its and its subsidiaries’ representatives to, cease any activities described above and any discussions or negotiations with any person or group that may have been ongoing with respect to any alternative proposals.

event_claims.description: From 12:01 a.m. New York time on September 6, 2013 until the effective time or earlier termination, the Company and related parties were required not to solicit or facilitate alternative proposals, subject to excluded-party and other permitted exceptions.
event_claims.quote_text: from 12:01 a.m., New York time, on September 6, 2013 until the effective time or, if earlier, the termination of the merger agreement, the Company has been and is required to not, and to cause its subsidiaries and its and their respective representatives not to, directly or indirectly:

event_claims.description: After the no-shop start, the Company and related parties were barred from initiating, soliciting, encouraging or facilitating inquiries, proposals or offers with respect to alternative proposals.
event_claims.quote_text: initiate, solicit, encourage or facilitate any inquiry, proposal or offer with respect to, or the making, submission or announcement of, any alternative proposal;

event_claims.description: After the no-shop start, the Company and related parties were barred from discussions or negotiations regarding alternative proposals or furnishing non-public information to potential alternative-proposal makers.
event_claims.quote_text: engage in, enter into, continue or otherwise participate in any discussions or negotiations regarding an alternative proposal with, or furnish any non-public information regarding the Company or its subsidiaries to, any person that has made or, to the Company’s knowledge, is considering making an alternative proposal.

event_claims.description: After the go-shop period and before shareholder approval, if the Company receives a written alternative proposal, the Company and its representatives may contact the proposing person to clarify terms and conditions.
event_claims.quote_text: if following 12:01 a.m., New York time, on September 6, 2013 and prior to obtaining the required vote of the Company’s shareholders to approve the merger agreement, the Company receives a written alternative proposal, the Company and its representatives may contact the person who has made the alternative proposal to clarify the terms and conditions thereof

event_claims.description: If the board determines a post-go-shop alternative proposal constitutes or could reasonably be expected to result in a superior proposal, the Company may provide information and access under qualifying confidentiality agreements.
event_claims.quote_text: provide information (including non-public information or data) regarding, and afford access to, the business, properties, assets, books, records and personnel of, the Company and its subsidiaries pursuant to confidentiality agreements meeting certain requirements

event_claims.description: If the board determines a post-go-shop alternative proposal constitutes or could reasonably be expected to result in a superior proposal, the Company may participate in discussions or negotiations with the proposer.
event_claims.quote_text: engage in, enter into, continue or otherwise participate in discussions or negotiations with any person with respect to the alternative proposal.

event_claims.description: Since 12:01 a.m. New York time on September 6, 2013, the Company was required to notify Hudson’s Bay of qualifying confidentiality agreements, alternative proposals, or proposals that could reasonably be expected to result in alternative proposals.
event_claims.quote_text: Since 12:01 a.m., New York time, on September 6, 2013, the Company has been and is required to promptly (and in any event within 48 hours) notify Hudson’s Bay of its entry into a confidentiality agreement meeting certain requirements or its receipt of an alternative proposal or amendment thereto, or a proposal or offer that could

event_claims.description: Before shareholder approval, if the Company receives an alternative proposal determined to be a superior proposal, the board may terminate the merger agreement to enter into an acquisition agreement for that superior proposal, subject to merger-agreement requirements.
event_claims.quote_text: if, prior to obtaining the required vote of the Company’s shareholders to approve the merger agreement, the Company receives an alternative proposal that the board determines in good faith after consultation with its outside counsel and financial advisors constitutes a superior proposal

event_claims.description: The board may, subject to requirements including notice and Hudson’s Bay match rights, terminate the merger agreement to enter into an acquisition agreement for a superior proposal.
event_claims.quote_text: the board may, subject to compliance with obligations set forth in the merger agreement, including providing Hudson’s Bay with prior notice and allowing Hudson’s Bay the right to negotiate with the Company to match the terms of any superior proposal, terminate the merger agreement to enter into an acquisition agreement with respect to such superior proposal, subject to payment of the Company termination fee
